title: ZKP Payment Authorization for AWS AgentCore Agents
description: AWS AgentCore + Stripe + Privy enables agent payments but lacks fine-grained spending authorization proofs. Bolyra's FINANCIAL_SMALL/MEDIUM/UNLIMITED permission tiers and delegation circuit could provide cryptographic spending caps that agents prove on-chain before transacting — no trust in the orchestrator required.

title: Machine-Native Identity Layer for Agentic Wallets
description: The 2026 shift to agentic wallets (Privy, Coinbase, TON) creates demand for self-sovereign agent identity that isn't just a keypair. Bolyra's AgentPolicy proof bundles model hash, operator signature, and permission scope into a single verifiable credential — giving wallets reputation-building primitives without doxxing the operator.

title: LangChain Agent Authentication Plugin
description: LangChain lacks native agent-to-agent authentication. Bolyra's AgentPolicy circuit could provide a LangChain middleware that lets agents prove credentials (model hash, operator, permissions) without revealing private keys. The existing langchain integration in integrations/langchain/ is a natural launchpad.

title: DID-Native ZKP Credentials for Zero-Trust Agent Frameworks
description: The arxiv paper (2505.19301) validates the zero-trust + DID + VC direction for agentic AI but uses conventional VCs without ZKP selective disclosure. Bolyra's did:bolyra method and proof envelope format are purpose-built for this gap — agents prove specific claims (e.g., 'authorized for financial ops under $10K') without revealing the full credential.

title: ZKP-Based Role Verification for AutoGen Multi-Agent Systems
description: AutoGen's discussion #4656 shows teams struggling with agent authorization in multi-agent orchestrations. Bolyra's cumulative-bit permissions and Delegation circuit map directly to role-based access control where agents prove capability tiers without exposing the full permission set. This is a pure protocol fit.

title: Auditable Agent Delegation Chains for Enterprise SaaS
description: As enterprises deploy agent swarms, the question 'who authorized this agent to act?' becomes a compliance requirement. Bolyra's Delegation circuit provides cryptographic proof of the full authorization chain (human → operator → agent → sub-agent) with one-way scope narrowing, satisfying auditors without revealing internal org structure.

title: Zero-Knowledge Non-Human Identity Attestations for Enterprise IAM
description: Okta is building centralized NHI governance but can't solve the privacy problem: enterprises don't want to reveal which agents they run or what permissions they hold to third-party identity providers. Bolyra's handshake protocol lets agents prove authorization without leaking internal policy to the IdP. Position as a privacy layer that sits beneath Okta/Entra.

title: Privacy-Preserving Cross-App Agent Access (XAA Alternative)
description: Okta's XAA protocol centralizes app-to-app agent connections under one identity fabric, creating a single point of trust and surveillance. Bolyra's mutual ZKP handshake offers a decentralized alternative where agents prove authorization across app boundaries without a central broker seeing every connection. Target enterprises with multi-vendor agent deployments.

title: Privacy-Preserving Agent Reputation via Nullifier-Based History
description: Agentic wallets need reputation but agents can't build track records without linking transactions to a persistent identity. Bolyra's nullifier mechanism (from HumanUniqueness, adapted for agents) enables unlinkable yet consistent reputation scores — an agent proves 'I have completed N transactions above threshold' without revealing which ones.
